mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.0% is near the natural rate. A declining trend in UNRATE signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumption and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window is below 0.03, or the 20-day forward return in periods of falling UNRATE is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) trending above 2.5% year-over-year triggers market expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When triggered, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative, or the proportion of negative forward returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand and residential investment. This joint signal of expansionary household activity supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: In periods when both HOUST and RSAFS are above their 12-month moving averages, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 100 (the 2017 base index level), it indicates contraction in the manufacturing sector. In such a regime, forward equity returns are negative as declining output signals recession risk.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is below 100, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative on average, or the negative return proportion is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, the primary driver of U.S. GDP. An always-on positive trend in RSAFS supports corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) increase exceeding 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market. Such strong job gains boost consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When triggered, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not positive, or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) characterizes late-cycle monetary tightening. This joint condition increases recession risk and depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month average and UNRATE is below its 12-month average, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Following the end of a recession, rapid GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a strong recovery. In this regime, positive GDP surprise drives upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: In recovery periods (as identified by NBER dating), the 60-day forward S&P 500 return shows a negative average or a hit rate below 60%.
